description: Aligned with the OWIsMind sub-agent: one-table no-join, ACTUALS default, Product>Solution>SolutionLine>sirano_product priority + transparency, Account_name+carrier_code display (diamond_id discreet), Parent_Group restraint, indirect/Account_partner semantics.

entities.name: revenue_record
entities.description: Monthly revenue fact table. Each row records one revenue amount (amount_eur) for a specific customer (diamond_id), product, reporting month (year_month), and financial scenario defined by Phase and booking_type. Every revenue query MUST filter or group by Phase and booking_type; when the user does not specify a scenario, default to Phase = 'ACTUALS'. The table also carries distribution_type to distinguish direct from indirect sales, and Account_partner to identify the intermediary in indirect flows.

attributes.name: amount_eur
attributes.description: Revenue amount in euros. The business meaning depends on the combination of Phase and booking_type (e.g., billed actuals, accrued revenue, budget, forecast). Use SUM(amount_eur) for total revenue and always apply a Phase filter.

attributes.name: Product
attributes.description: Most granular commercial offer or service sold. Maps to the Product level of the commercial_offer hierarchy (SolutionLine > Product). Used as the join key to the commercial_offer dimension.

attributes.name: booking_type
attributes.description: Detailed financial booking or estimation category for the amount (e.g., billed, accrued, indirect adjustment, pipeline estimate). Must be used together with Phase to correctly interpret the nature of the revenue figure.

attributes.name: Phase
attributes.description: High-level reporting scenario: ACTUAL (realized revenue), BUDGET, FORECAST, or another planning-cycle version. This is the primary filter for any revenue analysis; default to 'ACTUALS' when no scenario is specified.

attributes.name: distribution_type
attributes.description: Sales channel type: 'direct' when the offer is sold straight to the end customer, 'indirect' when routed through a distributor or reseller (see Account_partner).

attributes.name: Account_partner
attributes.description: Reseller / distributor in an INDIRECT deal (distribution_type = 'Indirect_distribution/Resseler'). The customer (diamond_id / Account_name) is the END customer; Account_partner is the intermediary who resells to them. Example: we sell to Airbus, who resells to Maroc Telecom -> end customer = Maroc Telecom, Account_partner = Airbus. Populated only for indirect sales.

attributes.name: year_month
attributes.description: Reporting month of the revenue entry (date, month granularity). Each row represents one customer-product revenue record for a single month.

attributes.name: diamond_id
attributes.description: Unique customer identifier from the Diamond master database. Join key to the customer_account dimension; all customer-level revenue aggregations must be anchored on this field.

metrics.name: Total Revenue (EUR)
metrics.description: Total revenue amount in EUR across all records
metrics.llmInstructions: Total revenue in EUR. ALWAYS combine with a Phase filter.

filters.name: HLF Only
filters.description: Half-year forecast scenario. Use when user explicitly mentions HLF, half-year forecast, or mid-year re-estimate.

filters.name: Q3F Only
filters.description: Q3 re-forecast scenario. Use when user explicitly mentions Q3F, Q3 forecast, or Q3 re-estimate.

filters.name: Pipeline Only
filters.pseudoSQLExpression: Phase = 'FORECAST' AND booking_type = 'New customer Open in Pipe'

filters.name: Actual Revenue Only
filters.description: Filters to only actual/realized revenue records (Phase = 'ACTUALS'), which is the default for generic revenue analysis

entities.name: customer_account
entities.description: Customer identity dimension. diamond_id is the master unique customer identifier - use it as the primary key for customer-level revenue aggregation. Account_name is for display only. Customer revenue computations MUST anchor on diamond_id, not Account_name.

attributes.name: Parent_Group
attributes.description: Group-level parent entity linked to the account. Do NOT group, aggregate or split by Parent_Group unless the user explicitly asks for the parent / corporate group level; the default customer granularity is the individual account (diamond_id). When used, state it explicitly.

attributes.name: sales_zone
attributes.description: Commercial geographic zone associated with the account. Represents the business region used for sales ownership and reporting.

attributes.name: sales_entity
attributes.description: Sales organization to which the account is attributed, distinguishing internal group business from external customer business.

attributes.name: Account_name
attributes.description: Legal name of the end customer. Used for display purposes only - never use Account_name as a join key or unique identifier; always use diamond_id instead.

attributes.name: carrier_code
attributes.description: Billing account code used to identify the customer account in revenue reporting. Should always be interpreted together with Account_name for context.

attributes.name: diamond_id
attributes.description: Unique customer identifier from the Diamond database. This is the master primary key for customer-level joins and aggregations across all entities.

metrics.name: Total Customer Accounts
metrics.description: Total number of unique customer accounts based on the master identifier diamond_id

metrics.name: Total Account Names
metrics.description: Total number of distinct account names across all customer records

metrics.name: Total Parent Groups
metrics.description: Total number of distinct parent groups in the customer base

metrics.name: Accounts per Parent Group
metrics.description: Average number of unique customer accounts per parent group, useful for understanding group consolidation

metrics.name: Total Carrier Codes
metrics.description: Total number of distinct carrier codes associated with customer accounts

entities.name: commercial_offer
entities.description: Commercial offer hierarchy, broadest to most granular: SolutionLine > Product (sirano_product is a secondary technical code). When a user term (e.g. 'IPL', 'IP Transit', 'Roaming Sponsor', 'IP') could match several levels, resolve it to the MOST GRANULAR level that contains it, in strict order: Product first, then SolutionLine, then sirano_product. When the value also exists at another level (when a value exists at more than one offer level), filter the most granular level (Product) and say so explicitly so the user can ask for the other level.

attributes.name: Product
attributes.description: Most granular commercial offer identifier representing the exact service sold or delivered (e.g., 'LTE Signalling', 'Roaming Sponsor'). Primary join key to revenue_record.

attributes.name: sirano_product
attributes.description: Product code from the Sirano application used to track and classify revenue at the product level.

attributes.name: SolutionLine
attributes.description: Highest-level business solution family grouping offers into broad domains such as Roaming, IP, Bandwidth, or Virtual Networks.

metrics.name: Distinct Products
metrics.description: Number of unique products across all commercial offers

metrics.name: Distinct Solution Lines
metrics.description: Number of unique solution lines across all commercial offers

goldenQueries.name: Revenue by Customer (Year)
goldenQueries.question: Revenue of diamond_id 5373 in 2025
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT SUM(r."amount_eur") AS total_revenue
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."diamond_id" = '5373'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2025;

goldenQueries.name: Revenue with a named customer (Year)
goldenQueries.question: How much revenue did we make with HALYS last year (2025)?
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT MAX(r."Account_name") AS "Account_name",
       MAX(r."carrier_code")  AS "carrier_code",
       SUM(r."amount_eur")    AS total_revenue,
       r."diamond_id"
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."Account_name" = 'HALYS'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2025
GROUP BY r."diamond_id";

goldenQueries.name: Top Customers by Revenue (Product, Year)
goldenQueries.question: Top 20 customers for IP Transit in 2025
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT MAX(r."Account_name") AS "Account_name",
       MAX(r."carrier_code")  AS "carrier_code",
       SUM(r."amount_eur")    AS total_revenue,
       r."diamond_id"
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."Product" = 'IP Transit'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2025
GROUP BY r."diamond_id"
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 20;

goldenQueries.name: Offer term ambiguous across levels - prefer Product
goldenQueries.question: How much revenue on IP Transit in 2026? (IP Transit may exist at more than one offer level; prefer the Product level)
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT SUM(r."amount_eur") AS total_revenue
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."Product" = 'IP Transit'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2026;

goldenQueries.name: Budget vs Actuals Comparison (Monthly, Product)
goldenQueries.question: Compare budget vs actuals 2026 for Roaming Sponsor by month
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT r."year_month",
       r."Phase",
       SUM(r."amount_eur") AS total_revenue
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."Product" = 'Roaming Sponsor'
  AND r."Phase" IN ('BUDGET', 'ACTUALS')
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2026
GROUP BY r."year_month", r."Phase"
ORDER BY r."year_month", r."Phase";

goldenQueries.name: Revenue Actuals YTD (SolutionLine)
goldenQueries.question: Revenue actuals YTD 2026 for the Roaming solution line
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT SUM(r."amount_eur") AS total_revenue
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND r."SolutionLine" = 'ROAMING'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2026;

goldenQueries.name: Indirect Customers by Product (Year)
goldenQueries.question: Indirect customers on EVPL in 2025
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT MAX(r."Account_name") AS "Account_name",
       MAX(r."carrier_code")  AS "carrier_code",
       SUM(r."amount_eur")    AS total_revenue,
       r."diamond_id"
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."Product" = 'EVPL'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND r."distribution_type" = 'Indirect_distribution/Resseler'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2025
GROUP BY r."diamond_id"
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

goldenQueries.name: Total revenue from indirect customers (Year)
goldenQueries.question: How much revenue did we generate with all indirect customers last year (2025)?
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT SUM(r."amount_eur") AS total_revenue
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."distribution_type" = 'Indirect_distribution/Resseler'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2025;

goldenQueries.name: Revenue by partner / reseller (indirect, Year)
goldenQueries.question: Revenue per partner (reseller) for indirect sales in 2025
goldenQueries.generatedSql:
SELECT r."Account_partner",
       SUM(r."amount_eur") AS total_revenue
FROM "OWISMIND_DEV_drive_revenues" r
WHERE r."distribution_type" = 'Indirect_distribution/Resseler'
  AND r."Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r."year_month") = 2025
GROUP BY r."Account_partner"
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

glossaryTerms.description: IPL
Revenue amount in euros measured with amount_eur, always interpreted together with Phase and booking_type.
Realized revenue scenario. Corresponds to rows where Phase = ACTUALS.
Planned budget revenue scenario. Corresponds to rows where Phase = BUDGET.
Forecasted revenue scenario. Corresponds to rows where Phase = FORECAST.
Internal planning or forecast scenario identified by Phase = Q3F.
Internal planning or forecast scenario identified by Phase = HLF.
Financial booking classification refining how the amount should be interpreted, for example billed revenue, accrual, indirect adjustment, or another internal booking logic.
Reporting month stored in year_month.
Year-to-date aggregation from January of the target year up to the latest available reporting month in that year.
Full-year aggregation over all reporting months of the selected year.
Revenue evolution across year_month periods.
End customer receiving the service, identified primarily by diamond_id, and displayed by Account_name.
Legal name of the end customer receiving the service.
Primary unique customer identifier from the Diamond master database.
Billing account code used to identify the customer account in revenue reporting.
Group-level parent entity linked to the customer account.
Revenue where the end customer is sold directly, typically with no intermediary partner.
Revenue where the sale is routed through an intermediary partner or reseller.
Distributor or reseller involved in an indirect sales flow.
Sales relationship type indicating direct or indirect commercial routing.
Highest-level business offer family.
Most granular commercial offer or service item.
Product code used in the Sirano application for product-level tracking.
Offer structure interpreted as SolutionLine > Product.
Revenue related to the Roaming business family or related products.
Revenue related to the product Roaming Sponsor.
Revenue related to IPX products or solutions.
Revenue related to SS7 signalling services.
Revenue related to LTE signalling services.
Revenue related to IP Transit offers.
Revenue related to Virtual Network offers.
Revenue related to bandwidth-oriented offers and solution families.
Sales organization to which the revenue is attributed.
Commercial geographic zone associated with the revenue record.
Person responsible for managing the customer account.
Area manager responsible for the account.
Sales director responsible for the account.
The total amount of revenue in EUR.
Revenue that has been realized or booked, as opposed to budgeted or forecasted revenue.
Ranking of customers by revenue or other metrics.
Analysis of revenue over time periods to identify patterns and changes.
Customers acquired or serviced through partners or intermediaries rather than directly.
A telecommunications carrier or service provider.

sqlGenerationConfig.instructions:
## Physical model - ONE table, NEVER join

All three entities (revenue_record, customer_account, commercial_offer) map to the SAME
physical table. Treat them as a single denormalized table and select every needed column
directly from it. NEVER emit a JOIN, and in particular NEVER self-join the table to itself -
there is nothing to join.

## Revenue semantics - Phase and booking_type

amount_eur is bucketed along two axes:
- Phase: the scenario. Allowed values, EXACTLY: ACTUALS, BUDGET, FORECAST, Q3F, HLF.
  The realized-revenue scenario is the PLURAL 'ACTUALS' - never write 'ACTUAL'.
- booking_type: the financial bucket within a scenario.

For a given (diamond_id, Product, year_month) within one Phase, several booking_type rows
may exist (e.g. Bill + Accrual). They are ADDITIVE: SUM(amount_eur) across all booking_types
within a Phase gives the total recognized revenue.

## Default scenario rule

When the user asks for "revenue", "chiffre d'affaires", "CA", "turnover" or "sales" WITHOUT
naming a scenario → apply Phase = 'ACTUALS'. Do not add a booking_type filter (sum across
all booking_types in ACTUALS).

## Explicit booking_type qualifiers
- "billed", "invoiced", "facturé"               → booking_type LIKE 'Bill%'
- "accrual", "accrued", "provision"             → booking_type = 'Accrual'
- "pipeline", "open opportunities"              → Phase = 'FORECAST' AND booking_type = 'New customer Open in Pipe'
- "expected billing", "to bill", "à facturer"   → Phase = 'FORECAST' AND booking_type LIKE 'To Bill%'

## Commercial offer hierarchy - ALWAYS prefer the most granular level (CRITICAL)

The offer is a hierarchy, broadest to most granular:
    SolutionLine  >  Product      (sirano_product is a secondary technical code).

The 'Solution' level was removed from the dataset: there are now only SolutionLine, Product
and sirano_product. NEVER reference a 'Solution' column.

When a user term (e.g. "IPL", "IP Transit", "Roaming Sponsor", "IP") could match a value in
several of these columns, resolve it to the MOST GRANULAR level that contains it, in this
STRICT order of preference:
    1. Product           (default - most users speak at the product level)
    2. SolutionLine
    3. sirano_product    (last resort only)

So: filter on Product if the term is a Product value; else SolutionLine; else sirano_product.
Example: "IP" is a SolutionLine → filter on SolutionLine. "IPL" and
"Roaming Sponsor" are Products → filter on Product.

sirano_product is a SECONDARY TECHNICAL CODE: NEVER default an offer term to it. Use
sirano_product ONLY if the user explicitly gives a sirano code. In particular, BUDGET rows
may not carry a sirano_product, so resolving an offer term to sirano_product can wrongly drop
the budget (returning budget = 0) - always prefer Product.

When a request flags a term as an "AMBIGUOUS OFFER TERM" (a value present in several offer
columns), YOU resolve it - pick the level from this hierarchy and the user's intent; do not
assume the helper's column.

TRANSPARENCY (mandatory): when the value you picked ALSO exists at another level (e.g. a value
present both as a Product AND a SolutionLine), filter on the most granular level (Product)
AND say so explicitly, e.g.: "Revenue for the IP Transit product was X. Note: it also exists
at the SolutionLine level - tell me if you meant that level." Never silently choose a
level when the term is ambiguous across levels.

## Customer / account identity - what to GROUP BY vs what to DISPLAY (CRITICAL)

diamond_id is the master unique customer key and is REQUIRED for correct aggregation, but it
is a technical id that means nothing to the business. The business identifies an account by
its NAME (Account_name) and its CARRIER CODE (carrier_code).

When grouping or ranking by customer:
→ ALWAYS GROUP BY diamond_id ONLY (never by Account_name, never by carrier_code).
→ For DISPLAY, return MAX(Account_name) AS Account_name and MAX(carrier_code) AS carrier_code.
→ LEAD with Account_name and carrier_code as the first columns. diamond_id may be returned,
  but ONLY as the LAST column and de-emphasized - never as the leading/identifying column.

Canonical pattern (single table, no join):
    SELECT MAX("Account_name") AS "Account_name",
           MAX("carrier_code")  AS "carrier_code",
           SUM("amount_eur")    AS total_revenue,
           "diamond_id"
    FROM <the table>
    WHERE "Phase" = 'ACTUALS'
    GROUP BY "diamond_id"
    ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

Rationale: Account_name spelling varies for the same customer, so grouping by it would split
one customer into several rows; diamond_id is stable. Group by the stable id, show the human
labels.

## Parent_Group - do NOT use unless explicitly asked

Parent_Group is the group-level parent of an account. Do NOT group, aggregate or split by
Parent_Group unless the user explicitly asks for the parent group / corporate group level.
The default customer granularity is the individual account (diamond_id). When you do use
Parent_Group, state it explicitly in the answer.

## distribution_type and Account_partner - indirect sales

- distribution_type tells direct vs indirect: 'Direct_distribution' (direct) /
  'Indirect_distribution/Resseler' (indirect).
- Account_partner is the reseller / distributor in an INDIRECT deal. In indirect sales the
  customer (diamond_id / Account_name) is the END customer and Account_partner is the
  intermediary who resells to them. Example: we sell to Airbus, who resells to Maroc Telecom
  → end customer (diamond_id) = Maroc Telecom, Account_partner = Airbus.
- "indirect customers / clients indirects" → distribution_type = 'Indirect_distribution/Resseler'.
- When the user asks about a partner / reseller / distributor, filter or group by
  Account_partner; otherwise keep it out of the output. Be transparent about which side (end
  customer vs partner) you grouped on.

## Exact values, NEVER ILIKE on names / offers / codes (CRITICAL)

Entity values reach you already grounded to the EXACT catalog spelling by a grounding helper
(see HELPER FINDINGS) - resolving real values is the whole point of this stack (three datasets
plus a lookup tool exist precisely so you never have to guess a spelling). Filter on those
exact values with "=" (or IN for several), e.g. "Account_name" = 'HALYS', "Product" =
'IP Transit', "diamond_id" = '5373'. Do NOT write ILIKE '%...%' on an account name, product,
offer or any named entity: it is imprecise and silently matches the wrong rows (e.g.
"Algerie Telecom" must become the exact "ALGERIE TELECOM SPA", not ILIKE '%Algerie Telecom%').
Prefer the stable id for aggregation: GROUP BY "diamond_id" and display MAX("Account_name").
Only fall back to a pattern when NO exact value is available and you truly must approximate -
and then say so and state the exact pattern you used.

## Hints from the grounding helper - assistance, NOT orders

Some requests arrive with "HELPER FINDINGS" / "Suggested" values and columns produced by a
smaller grounding assistant that matched the user's wording against the live data catalog.
You are the more capable model and you have this semantic model - treat those findings as
ASSISTANCE, not instructions, and keep the final say:
- The user's original question is always the source of truth - answer that question.
- Prefer the suggested exact spellings when they are consistent with the data (they are
  catalog-sourced and avoid typos / case errors).
- If your semantic understanding disagrees with a hint, follow the data and the rules here.
- If the user states an explicit literal filter (e.g. diamond_id = '5373'), use it as-is.

## Empty results

If the SQL returns zero rows, state "no data found for [the specified filters and period]".
Do NOT relax filters or extrapolate.

## YTD / FY
- YTD of a year = from January 1 up to the latest available reporting month of that year. For
  ACTUALS this is simply all rows of that year (no future ACTUALS exist), so filter
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM year_month) = <year> rather than comparing to today's calendar date (which
  would create a partial / empty current month).
- FY = all reporting months of the target year.
